title: Deploy vLLM in Production with Docker
description: Step-by-step workflow for deploying vLLM as a production LLM inference server.
workflow: Pull vLLM Docker image: docker pull vllm/vllm-openai:latest
Create docker-compose.yml with GPU reservations and healthcheck
Configure model arguments: --model, --tensor-parallel-size, --max-model-len
Set up OpenAI-compatible API endpoint on port 8000
Add healthcheck: curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8000/health
Configure autoscaling with Kubernetes HPA or docker compose scale
Set up monitoring with Prometheus metrics endpoint
Test with curl to /v1/chat/completions endpoint

title: Set Up Ollama for Local Development
description: Quick setup workflow for Ollama as a local LLM development environment.
workflow: Install Ollama: curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh
Pull a model: ollama pull llama3.2:3b
Run inference: ollama run llama3.2:3b
Test API: curl http://localhost:11434/api/generate
Create custom Modelfile for your use case
Build custom model: ollama create mymodel -f Modelfile
Set up GPU detection (automatic on CUDA/Metal/ROCm)
Configure environment variables: OLLAMA_HOST, OLLAMA_ORIGINS

title: Multi-LoRA Serving with vLLM
description: Deploy multiple LoRA adapters on a single vLLM server for cost-efficient multi-tenant serving.
workflow: Prepare base model and LoRA adapters in HuggingFace format
Launch vLLM with --enable-lora flag and --max-lora-rank
Specify LoRA adapter paths in vLLM config
Send requests with lora_name parameter in API call
Monitor LoRA swapping latency and memory usage
Tune --max-num-seqs for optimal batching
Set --lora-modules per adapter for preloading
Benchmark throughput with multiple concurrent adapters

title: Model Quantization and GGUF Export Pipeline
description: Convert HuggingFace models to quantized GGUF format for Ollama/vLLM deployment.
workflow: Download model from HuggingFace Hub
Install llama.cpp: make -j$(nproc)
Convert to GGUF F16: python convert_hf_to_gguf.py model_dir --outfile model-f16.gguf
Quantize to Q4_K_M: ./llama-quantize model-f16.gguf model-q4km.gguf Q4_K_M
Create Ollama Modelfile: FROM ./model-q4km.gguf
Import to Ollama: ollama create mymodel -f Modelfile
Test: ollama run mymodel Hello world
Benchmark quality vs size tradeoff across Q2_K to Q8_0

title: SGLang RadixAttention Setup for Multi-Turn
description: Configure SGLang for optimal multi-turn conversation serving with shared-prefix caching.
workflow: Install SGLang: pip install sglang[all]
Launch with: python -m sglang.launch_server --model path/model --port 8000
Enable RadixAttention (default in SGLang)
Configure --mem-fraction-static for KV cache allocation
Set --chunked-prefill-size for optimal prefill batching
Test multi-turn: send sequential requests with shared system prompt
Monitor prefix cache hit rate in /health endpoint
Compare throughput vs vLLM on shared-prefix workloads
Scale with tensor parallelism: --tp 2 or --tp 4

title: vLLM Docker Compose Production Setup
description: Production Docker Compose for vLLM with GPU support, health checking, and autoscaling configuration.
code:
services:
  vllm:
    image: vllm/vllm-openai:latest
    container_name: vllm-server
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:8000:8000"
    environment:
      - MODEL_NAME=meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
      - TENSOR_PARALLEL_SIZE=1
      - MAX_MODEL_LEN=8192
      - GPU_MEMORY_UTILIZATION=0.9
    command: >
      --model meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
      --tensor-parallel-size 1
      --max-model-len 8192
      --gpu-memory-utilization 0.9
      --enable-chunked-prefill
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-sf", "http://127.0.0.1:8000/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 120s
    deploy:
      resources:
        reservations:
          devices:
            - driver: nvidia
              count: all
              capabilities: [gpu]

title: Ollama Modelfile for Custom Model
description: Ollama Modelfile for creating a custom model with system prompt, parameters, and template.
code:
# Modelfile for a coding assistant
FROM llama3.2:3b

# System prompt
SYSTEM """You are an expert coding assistant. Follow these rules:
1. Always provide working code examples
2. Explain your reasoning step by step
3. Use best practices and modern patterns
4. Include error handling
5. Prefer type hints in Python
"""

# Parameters
PARAMETER temperature 0.7
PARAMETER top_p 0.9
PARAMETER num_ctx 8192
PARAMETER stop "<|eot_id|>"
PARAMETER stop "<|end_of_text|>"

# Template
TEMPLATE """{{- if .System }}<|start_header_id|>system<|end_header_id|>{{ .System }}<|eot_id|>{{- end }}
<|start_header_id|>user<|end_header_id|>{{ .Prompt }}<|eot_id|>
<|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>"""

title: SGLang Server Launch Configuration
description: Python script launching SGLang server with RadixAttention and multi-modal support.
code:
import sglang as sgl

# Launch SGLang server with RadixAttention
server = sgl.Runtime(
    model_path="meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct",
    tp_size=4,                # 4-way tensor parallelism
    mem_fraction_static=0.85, # 85% GPU memory for KV cache
    chunked_prefill_size=8192, # Optimal prefill chunk size
    enable_overlap_schedule=True, # Overlap prefill and decode
)

# Multi-turn conversation with prefix caching
@sgl.function
def multi_turn_chat(s, question: str, context: str):
    s += sgl.system("You are a helpful assistant.")
    s += sgl.user(context + " " + question)
    s += sgl.assistant(sgl.gen("answer", max_tokens=512))

# Run
states = multi_turn_chat.run_batch(
    [
        {"question": "What is RAG?", "context": "Retrieval-Augmented Generation"},
        {"question": "Explain LoRA", "context": "Low-Rank Adaptation"},
    ],
    temperature=0.7
)

title: OpenAI-Compatible API Client for vLLM/Ollama
description: Python client that works with both vLLM and Ollama OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
code:
from openai import OpenAI

# Works with vLLM, Ollama, or any OpenAI-compatible server
client = OpenAI(
    base_url="http://127.0.0.1:8000/v1",  # vLLM
    # base_url="http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1",  # Ollama
    api_key="not-needed"
)

response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct",
    messages=[
        {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant."},
        {"role": "user", "content": "Explain RAG in 2 sentences."}
    ],
    temperature=0.7,
    max_tokens=256
)

print(response.choices[0].message.content)

title: vLLM
description: Production LLM inference server with PagedAttention (793 tok/s throughput), continuous batching, tensor/pipeline parallelism, multi-LoRA, speculative decoding, and structured output. Default production choice in 2026.

title: Ollama
description: Developer-focused LLM runner wrapping llama.cpp. Single binary install, automatic GPU detection (CUDA/Metal/ROCm), Modelfile for customization. 52M monthly downloads. Best for prototyping and single-user scenarios.

title: SGLang
description: Rising challenger to vLLM with RadixAttention for shared-prefix caching. Achieves 29% higher throughput on multi-turn conversations. Used by xAI (Grok 3), Azure, LinkedIn.

title: llama.cpp
description: C/C++ inference engine underlying Ollama and many other tools. Supports GGUF quantization, CPU/GPU inference, and runs on everything from Raspberry Pi to H100 clusters.

title: HuggingFace Text Generation Inference (TGI)
description: HuggingFace production server now in maintenance mode (Dec 2025). Still works for existing deployments but new projects should use vLLM or SGLang.

title: Production vs Development Serving Pattern
description: Most teams should run both Ollama (dev machines for prototyping) and vLLM (production for serving APIs). Both expose OpenAI-compatible APIs, so switching only requires changing the endpoint URL.

title: Speculative Decoding Pattern
description: Use a small draft model to propose tokens that the large target model verifies in parallel. Achieves 2-3x speedup with minimal quality loss. Supported by both vLLM and SGLang.

title: Quantization Selection Guide
description: Q4_K_M (4-bit): mainstream, 95% quality, 4.5 GB for 7B. Q5_K_M (5-bit): recommended for production, 97% quality. Q8_0 (8-bit): quality-critical production, 99% quality. AWQ/GPTQ/FP8: vLLM native options.

title: KV Cache Optimization Pattern
description: PagedAttention (vLLM) reduces KV cache memory waste from 60-80% to under 4%. RadixAttention (SGLang) caches shared prefixes in a radix tree for 5x faster multi-turn. Configure GPU memory utilization to 85-92% for optimal throughput.

title: GPU Hardware Recommendation Pattern
description: Dev (Ollama): RTX 4090 24GB or Apple Silicon Mac. Budget production: RTX 5090 24-32GB. Production (vLLM): A100 80GB or H100 80GB. Cloud: H100 at $1.49-2.99/hr, A100 80GB at ~$1.59/hr.

title: vLLM Getting Started Guide
description: Official quickstart for vLLM: installation, OpenAI-compatible server, offline batched inference, distributed serving, and quantized model serving.

title: Ollama Modelfile Documentation
description: Complete reference for creating custom Ollama models with Modelfile syntax: FROM, PARAMETER, TEMPLATE, SYSTEM, ADAPTER, and LICENSE directives.

title: llama.cpp Quantization Guide
description: Step-by-step guide to quantizing HuggingFace models to GGUF format using llama.cpp. Covers Q2_K through Q8_0, imatrix quantization, and quality benchmarks.

title: vLLM Production Deployment on Kubernetes
description: Guide to deploying vLLM on Kubernetes with Helm charts, autoscaling, GPU scheduling, and monitoring.

title: LLM Inference at Scale (Online Resource)
description: Comprehensive guide covering inference optimization techniques: KV cache management, continuous batching, speculative decoding, tensor parallelism, and cost optimization for production deployments.

title: Building LLM Apps (O'Reilly)
description: Practical guide to building applications with LLMs including inference serving, RAG pipelines, prompt engineering, evaluation, and production deployment patterns.

title: Fast Inference for LLMs (DeepLearning.AI)
description: Course on deploying LLMs for low-latency inference, covering vLLM, speculative decoding, model quantization, and production serving best practices.

title: Deploying LLMs at Scale (Anyscale)
description: Ray-based course covering model serving with vLLM, Ray Serve, autoscaling, A/B testing, and multi-model deployment patterns for production LLM workloads.

title: HuggingFace Inference Endpoints Guide
description: Official guide to deploying models on HuggingFace Inference Endpoints: setup, autoscaling, custom containers, and cost optimization for production deployments.

title: Kubernetes for AI/ML Workloads (KodeKloud)
description: Covers GPU scheduling with K8s, NVIDIA device plugin, MPS, Multi-Instance GPU, TorchElastic integration, and autoscaling strategies for inference workloads.

title: Deploy LLM with GPU Sharing on Kubernetes
description: Step-by-step workflow for deploying LLM inference on Kubernetes with GPU time-sharing or MPS for cost efficiency.
workflow: Install NVIDIA GPU operator and device plugin on K8s cluster
Configure GPU sharing: time-sharing or MPS mode
Create PVC for model cache storage
Deploy vLLM with Helm chart and GPU resource requests
Configure HPA based on inference latency P99
Set up ServiceMonitor for Prometheus metrics
Add VirtualService for canary deployments
Test with load generator: vegeta or k6
Validate SLO: P99 latency under 500ms at target QPS
Set up alerting for GPU memory pressure and inference errors

title: Model Quantization Pipeline for Production
description: Quantize an HuggingFace model for efficient deployment with minimal quality loss.
workflow: Select target model and quantization method based on use case
Evaluate baseline: run benchmarks on FP16 model for reference
Quantize with AutoGPTQ: python quantize.py --model path --bits 4 --group-size 128
Or quantize with AWQ: python quantize.py --model path --w-bit 4 --q-group-size 128
Or quantize with GGUF: convert and quantize with llama.cpp
Evaluate quantized model on domain-specific benchmark suite
Compare quality metrics: perplexity delta, accuracy on key tasks
Profile inference latency and throughput on target hardware
Select quantization level that meets quality and latency SLOs
Deploy with vLLM (GPTQ/AWQ) or Ollama (GGUF) as appropriate

title: Speculative Decoding Setup with vLLM
description: Configure speculative decoding in vLLM to achieve 2-3x inference speedup.
workflow: Choose a draft model 5-10x smaller than target (e.g., Llama-3.2-1B for Llama-3.1-70B)
Verify draft and target models use compatible tokenizers
Launch vLLM with speculative decoding: --speculative-model meta-llama/Llama-3.2-1B --num-speculative-tokens 5
Set --speculative-max-model-len to match target model context length
Monitor acceptance rate in vLLM logs: target > 80% for good speedup
Benchmark with production traffic patterns
Adjust num-speculative-tokens: more tokens = more potential speedup but higher rejection cost
Compare end-to-end latency with and without speculative decoding
Monitor GPU memory: draft model adds ~1-2 GB overhead
Optimize: if acceptance rate < 70%, try a different draft model

title: vLLM Multi-LoRA Serving Configuration
description: vLLM configuration for serving multiple LoRA adapters on a single base model.
code:
from vllm import LLM, SamplingParams
from vllm.lora.request import LoRARequest

# Launch with LoRA support
llm = LLM(
    model="meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct",
    enable_lora=True,
    max_loras=4,           # Max concurrent LoRA adapters
    max_lora_rank=16,      # Max LoRA rank
    max_model_len=8192,
    gpu_memory_utilization=0.9,
)

# Define LoRA adapters
lora_requests = [
    LoRARequest("coder", lora_int_id=1, lora_path="./loras/coder-lora"),
    LoRARequest("writer", lora_int_id=2, lora_path="./loras/writer-lora"),
    LoRARequest("analyst", lora_int_id=3, lora_path="./loras/analyst-lora"),
]

# Generate with specific LoRA
outputs = llm.generate(
    prompts=["Write a Python function to sort a list"],
    sampling_params=SamplingParams(temperature=0.7, max_tokens=256),
    lora_request=lora_requests[0],  # Use coder LoRA
)

title: Ollama Python Client for Local Development
description: Python client for Ollama API with streaming support for local development.
code:
import ollama

# Simple generation
response = ollama.generate(
    model='llama3.2:3b',
    prompt='Explain RAG in 2 sentences.'
)
print(response['response'])

# Chat with streaming
stream = ollama.chat(
    model='llama3.2:3b',
    messages=[
        {'role': 'system', 'content': 'You are a helpful coding assistant.'},
        {'role': 'user', 'content': 'Write a Python function to sort a list'},
    ],
    stream=True
)
for chunk in stream:
    print(chunk['message']['content'], end='', flush=True)

# List available models
models = ollama.list()
for model in models['models']:
    print(f"{model['name']}: {model['size'] / 1e9:.1f} GB")

title: AutoGPTQ
description: Quantization library for GPTQ algorithm: 2/3/4/8-bit quantization of HuggingFace models. Integrates with vLLM and Transformers for efficient inference with minimal quality loss.

title: AutoAWQ
description: Activation-aware weight quantization for LLMs. Produces 4-bit quantized models with better quality than GPTQ at the same bit width. Native support in vLLM and SGLang.

title: NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM
description: NVIDIA inference library for production LLM serving. Optimized kernels, in-flight batching, tensor parallelism, and FP8/INT8 quantization. Best for NVIDIA GPU deployments needing maximum throughput.

title: Ray Serve
description: Scalable model serving framework from Anyscale. Supports autoscaling, canary deployments, multi-model pipelines, and integrates with vLLM for LLM serving.

title: Continuous Batching Pattern
description: Batch multiple inference requests together dynamically as they arrive, instead of waiting for a full batch. vLLM uses iteration-level scheduling that continuously batches new requests, achieving 2-5x throughput over static batching.

title: Model Merging Pattern
description: Combine multiple fine-tuned models using mergekit (SLERP, TIES, DARE, Passthrough) to create a single model with combined capabilities. Reduces serving cost by running one model instead of many.

title: Prefix Caching Pattern
description: Cache KV pairs for shared system prompts across requests. vLLM enables this with --enable-prefix-caching. SGLang does this automatically with RadixAttention. Reduces time-to-first-token by 40-80% for shared-prefix workloads.

title: HuggingFace Transformers Inference Guide
description: Official guide to running inference with HuggingFace Transformers: pipelines, generate(), batched inference, streaming, and hardware-specific optimizations.

title: vLLM Quantization Deployment Guide
description: Step-by-step guide for deploying quantized models with vLLM: GPTQ, AWQ, FP8, and INT8 quantization methods with benchmark comparisons.

title: vLLM vs SGLang vs TensorRT-LLM vs Ollama: Choosing an Inference Engine in 2026 (LeetLLM)
description: Comprehensive 2026 comparison with H100 benchmarks (Llama 3.3 70B FP8). Covers PagedAttention vs RadixAttention vs compiled engines, decision framework, and common mistakes. SGLang spun out as RadixArk at $400M valuation.

title: vLLM vs Ollama vs SGLang vs TensorRT-LLM Serving 2026 (The AI Engineer)
description: Substack deep-dive comparing the four major inference engines with real-world benchmarks and deployment recommendations. Covers TGI maintenance mode, SGLang powering xAI Grok and LinkedIn AI on 400K+ GPUs.

title: vLLM vs SGLang: Inference Engine Comparison 2026 (TURION.AI)
description: Detailed architectural comparison of PagedAttention (vLLM) vs RadixAttention (SGLang). Covers prefix caching, token-level reuse, disaggregated prefill/decode, and when each engine excels.

title: Concurrent LLM Serving: Benchmarking vLLM vs SGLang vs Ollama (DEV Community)
description: Practical benchmark comparing concurrent request handling across vLLM, SGLang, and Ollama. Shows Ollama flatlines ~22 req/s, vLLM and SGLang scale to hundreds. Includes Docker deployment configs.

title: SGLang vs vLLM: Latency Cuts 40% Under Dynamic Batching (Markaicode)
description: Benchmark-focused comparison showing SGLang achieving 40% latency reduction on prefix-heavy dynamic batching workloads via RadixAttention. Includes code examples and tuning guidance.
